seo.title: Docker vs Docker Compose vs Podman
seo.description: A practical comparison of Docker, Docker Compose, and Podman for Docker & Containers.

content.intro: Compare Docker Compose and Kubernetes for container orchestration in small to medium team workflows, focusing on implementation effort, lock-in risk, and reliability trade-offs.

options.name: Docker Compose
options.tagline: Local development and simple multi-container apps
options.best_for: Single-machine development, quick prototyping, and non-clustered deployments

options.name: Kubernetes
options.best_for: Production-grade scalability, auto-scaling, and distributed systems

criteria.name: Ease of Setup
criteria.description: Time and complexity required to configure the system

criteria.name: Scalability
criteria.description: Ability to handle growth in containers and nodes

criteria.name: Community Support
criteria.description: Availability of documentation and third-party tools

criteria.name: Security Features
criteria.description: Built-in security controls and compliance tools

criteria.name: Resource Efficiency
criteria.description: CPU and memory usage for equivalent workloads

verdict.summary: Docker Compose excels in simplicity and speed for local development, while Kubernetes provides robust scalability and enterprise features at the cost of complexity. Choose Docker Compose for single-host workflows and Kubernetes for distributed systems requiring auto-scaling.

use_cases.reason: Lower resource usage and simpler configuration preserve development speed
Native support for horizontal pod autoscaling and distributed resilience

use_cases.scenario: Small team with limited DevOps expertise
use_cases.reason: Reduced implementation effort and learning curve
